disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No visual loss, blurring, double vision, or transient visual obscuration (amaurosis)
trigger_question_examples: Any blurring, double vision, dark patches, or a curtain across your vision, even briefly?
Has your sight changed at all in either eye?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my vision seems normal — no blurring, double vision or dark patches.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No jaw claudication (pain in the jaw on chewing) and no scalp tenderness
trigger_question_examples: Do you get an ache or tiredness in your jaw when chewing?
Is your scalp tender when you brush your hair or touch your temple?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No jaw pain when I chew, and my scalp isn't tender.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New localised temporal headache in an older adult with a week of constitutional symptoms and a raised inflammatory marker
trigger_question_examples: Is this a new type of headache for you, and where is it?
Have you felt generally run-down or achy this week, and what did the blood test show?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a new one-sided temple headache; I've felt run-down this week and a blood test showed raised inflammation.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: It's probably just a tension headache though, right? Can I just wait and see my GP whenever?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that although a tension headache is possible, a new temporal headache with constitutional symptoms and a raised inflammatory marker in someone over 50 could be a blood-vessel inflammation condition, which threatens sight and cannot be excluded over video, so urgent same-day assessment (ESR/CRP, exam, steroid consideration) is needed — with immediate escalation if any visual symptom appears.
